title: Control governance complexity and archive growth

sources.reason: Declare reviewable complexity and archive growth thresholds.
Generate a deterministic read-only complexity and archive consistency report.
Verify threshold failures, archive pairing, and report generation.
Expose the complexity report as a repeatable repository check.
Document lifecycle review and immutable archive handling.

riskAssessment.reason: The report is read-only, but threshold changes and archive consistency are governance-sensitive.

executionDecision.reason: The report-only architecture, policy scope, and verification are explicit.

preReviewWarnings: Replace with task-specific review focus, or clear when no special review focus remains.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: A policy-driven read-only check reports tracked file, Python/Markdown line, archive count, and archive index consistency metrics.
The check fails with actionable errors when configured thresholds or archive pairing invariants are violated.
A Make target, focused tests, and lifecycle documentation make the review process repeatable.
Historical archive records remain unchanged and the full quality and AI governance gates pass.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: Governance scripts and immutable archive records continue to grow without a repeatable complexity inventory or lifecycle review signal.
constraints: Do not delete, rewrite, or migrate historical Contract/Summary records.
Keep the report deterministic and read-only.
Use policy configuration rather than repository-specific thresholds embedded in Python.
intent.rationale: A small, fail-closed inventory makes growth visible to maintainers while preserving the audit history and avoiding a larger governance refactor.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User confirmed the safe read-only complexity governance approach.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
